source.title: Engineering Bacillus pumilus alkaline serine protease to increase its low-temperature proteolytic activity by directed evolution

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: The mesophilic alkaline serine protease DHAP from Bacillus pumilus BA06 has high catalytic efficiency at ~50 °C but low activity at low temperatures, limiting its industrial use (e.g., leather processing). The goal is to improve cold activity via directed evolution while maintaining thermostability.

section_gists.heading: Abstract
section_gists.gist: Directed evolution yielded seven DHAP variants with increased proteolytic activity at 15 °C. Most retained thermostability. Kinetic analysis showed increased kcat. Combined variants (P9S/K27Q, P9S/T162I) had ~5-fold higher caseinolytic activity at 15 °C without thermostability loss. Structural modeling suggested mechanisms.
process_signals: Directed evolution was used to improve cold activity.
Seven variants were obtained and characterized.
Combined variants further improved activity.
Trade-off between activity and stability was not always observed.

section_gists.heading: Background
section_gists.gist: Mesophilic alkaline serine proteases are industrially important but have low cold activity. Cold-adapted proteases are thermosensitive. Protein engineering can improve cold activity of mesophilic proteases. DHAP from B. pumilus BA06 has optimal activity at 55 °C, unsuitable for low-temperature processes. Previous site-directed mutagenesis improved DHAP performance.
process_signals: Industrial need for cold-active proteases.
Previous work: site-directed mutagenesis based on homology and modeling improved DHAP.
Hypothesis: directed evolution can yield variants with improved low-temperature activity.
failures_or_limits: wt DHAP optimal temperature is 55 °C, too high for some applications.

section_gists.heading: Materials
section_gists.gist: Substrates: AAPF-pN and casein. Enzymes and reagents for cloning and mutagenesis. Hosts: E. coli JM109 for library, B. subtilis WB600 for expression.

section_gists.heading: Construction of the mutation library
section_gists.gist: Error-prone PCR with dITP and DNA shuffling used to create library. >100,000 independent clones. Mutation frequency ~3 mutations/kb. Dominant mutations: C→T, A→G transitions.
process_signals: Library construction protocol: error-prone PCR with dITP, DNA shuffling.
Library size: >100,000 clones.
Mutation frequency evaluated by sequencing 21 clones.

section_gists.heading: Screening of the mutation library
section_gists.gist: Two-round screening: first on milk plates (halo size), then 96-well plates with activity assays at 50 °C and 15 °C, plus thermostability test (60 °C). 1500 clones from ~30,000 screened. Second round in test tubes yielded 36 clones. Sequencing revealed 24 single mutants, 8 double, 4 with ≥3 mutations.
process_signals: Screening strategy: milk plates → 96-well plates → test tubes.
Selection criteria: improved low-temperature activity and/or thermostability.
36 clones confirmed with improved cold activity.
failures_or_limits: Only clones with larger halos were selected, potentially missing subtle improvements.

section_gists.heading: Construction of combined variants
section_gists.gist: Three beneficial mutations (P9S, K27Q, T162I) were combined via site-directed mutagenesis (QuickChange) to generate double and triple mutants.
process_signals: Rational combination of beneficial mutations to further improve activity.
Hypothesis: additive or synergistic effects.

section_gists.heading: Enzyme activity assay
section_gists.gist: Caseinolytic activity assay (Folin method) at various temperatures. AAPF-pN hydrolysis monitored at 410 nm. Kinetic parameters (kcat, Km) determined by fitting to Michaelis-Menten equation.
process_signals: Activity assays used to characterize variants.
Kinetic analysis to understand catalytic efficiency changes.

section_gists.heading: Thermostability
section_gists.gist: Thermostability evaluated by incubating enzymes at 60 °C, measuring residual caseinolytic activity over time.
process_signals: Thermostability is a key constraint; trade-off with cold activity was monitored.

section_gists.heading: Homology modeling
section_gists.gist: Homology model of DHAP built using Swiss-Model with subtilisin (1scjA) as template. Used to interpret mutation effects.
process_signals: Structural modeling to rationalize observed activity changes.
failures_or_limits: Model accuracy limited by template identity.

section_gists.heading: Construction and evaluation of a mutation library
section_gists.gist: Library >100,000 clones. Mutation frequency ~3/kb. 20/21 clones mutated. Dominant transitions: C→T, A→G. Transversions rare.
process_signals: Library quality confirmed: high mutation frequency, diverse mutations.

section_gists.heading: Screening of the mutation library
section_gists.gist: ~30,000 clones screened. 1500 selected after first round. 36 confirmed with improved cold activity. Sequencing: 24 single mutants, 8 double, 4 with ≥3 mutations.
process_signals: Screening funnel: 30,000 → 1500 → 36.
Variants with single and multiple mutations identified.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: Directed evolution can generate DHAP variants with improved low-temperature activity.
Combining beneficial mutations can yield additive or synergistic improvements.
Enhanced cold activity does not necessarily compromise thermostability.
Structural modeling can explain mutation effects (e.g., P9S near active site, K27Q on surface).
uncertainties_to_resolve: Were any negative results (e.g., variants with reduced activity or stability) discarded during screening?
What is the structural basis for the improved cold activity? (Only briefly mentioned in abstract, details likely in later sections.)
Were there any unsuccessful combined variants not reported?
How generalizable is the finding that cold activity and thermostability can be integrated?

events.summary: Directed evolution can improve the cold activity of DHAP.
A random mutant library of DHAP was constructed using error-prone PCR with dITP and DNA shuffling, yielding >100,000 independent clones.
The mutation library was screened for improved cold activity using a two-round process: milk plate halo size, then 96-well plate assays at 50 °C and 15 °C, plus thermostability test at 60 °C.
Sequencing of 21 random clones revealed a mutation frequency of ~3 mutations/kb, with 87% transitions (C→T, A→G dominant).
Seven variants (P9S, A1G/K27Q, A38V, A116T, T162I, S182R, T243S) were obtained and showed increased proteolytic activity at 15 °C towards casein and synthetic peptide.
Except T243S, the thermostability of the variants did not decrease compared to wild-type.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers problem statement, hypothesis, library construction, screening, initial variant characterization, and kinetic analysis.

events.summary: Seven variants were selected for further assays after initial screening.
All seven variants showed enhanced caseinolytic activity at various temperatures compared to wild-type.
Four new variants were constructed by combining three beneficial amino acid substitutions: P9S, K27Q, and T162I.
Combined variants showed further improved caseinolytic activity compared to original substitutions.
Thermostability was evaluated; some variants showed improved stability, others reduced.
Homology modeling was performed to interpret experimental data, identifying catalytic triad.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains detailed experimental results, kinetic analysis, and structural modeling, directly supporting the paper's research evolution.

events.summary: Structural modeling of DHAP was performed to interpret experimental data.
Substitution sites were far from the active center.
Four substitution sites (P9, A38, T162, S182) were predicted to be exposed on the protein surface.
Substitution of P9 with serine may increase flexibility without loss of thermostability.
Loop region substitutions (A38, T162, S182) may increase catalytic turnover number but can decrease thermostability (trade-off).
Buried sites (K27, A116, T243) were analyzed; K27Q may change local electrostatic potential and increase cold activity.
Combined variants (P9S/K27Q, P9S/T162I) showed further increased caseinolytic activity at low temperature, with effects being accumulative.
Conclusion: enhancement of cold activity and thermostability can be integrated into a single variant.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk contains detailed structural analysis and interpretation of mutation effects, linking experimental results to structural models. It includes analysis of surface vs. buried mutations, flexibility, electrostatic changes, and trade-offs between activity and stability. The conclusion reinforces the main finding.
This chunk contains only reference list and journal submission information. No research evolution events are present.

event_graph.paper_main_problem: The mesophilic alkaline serine protease DHAP from Bacillus pumilus BA06 has high catalytic efficiency at ~50 °C but low activity at low temperatures, limiting its industrial use. The goal is to improve cold activity via directed evolution while maintaining thermostability.

canonical_events.summary: Directed evolution can improve the cold activity of DHAP.
A random mutant library of DHAP was constructed using error-prone PCR with dITP and DNA shuffling, yielding >100,000 independent clones.

The mutation library was screened for improved cold activity using a two-round process: milk plate halo size, then 96-well plate assays at 50 °C and 15 °C, plus thermostability test at 60 °C. ~30,000 clones screened, 1500 selected, 36 final clones.
Sequencing of 21 random clones revealed a mutation frequency of ~3 mutations/kb, with 87% transitions (C→T, A→G dominant).
Seven variants (P9S, A1G/K27Q, A38V, A116T, T162I, S182R, T243S) were obtained and showed increased proteolytic activity at 15 °C towards casein and synthetic peptide.
Except T243S, the thermostability of the variants did not decrease compared to wild-type.
All seven variants showed enhanced caseinolytic activity at various temperatures compared to wild-type. A1G/K27Q and T162I had 2.62- and 2.41-fold increases at 15 °C.
Four new variants were constructed by combining three beneficial amino acid substitutions: P9S, K27Q, and T162I via site-directed mutagenesis.
Combined variants showed further improved caseinolytic activity compared to original substitutions. P9S/K27Q and P9S/T162I had ~5-fold increase at 15 °C, 2.8-fold at 50 °C.
Thermostability was evaluated; some variants showed improved stability (T243S, K27Q/T162I), others reduced (S182R, P9S/K27Q/T162I).
Homology modeling of DHAP was performed to interpret experimental data, identifying catalytic triad (D32, H64, S221) and substitution sites far from active center.

Structural analysis: P9S may increase flexibility without thermostability loss; loop substitutions (A38, T162, S182) may increase kcat but can decrease thermostability; K27Q may change electrostatic potential and increase cold activity.

Conclusion: enhancement of cold activity and thermostability can be integrated into a single variant. Combined variants P9S/K27Q and P9S/T162I achieved ~5-fold increase in caseinolytic activity at 15 °C without thermostability loss.

edges.reason: The hypothesis leads to the design of a mutant library to test it.
The library construction enables the screening for improved variants.
Sequencing analysis evaluates the quality of the library used in screening.
Screening identifies seven variants with increased cold activity.
Thermostability validation confirms most variants retain stability.
Detailed activity analysis quantifies the improvements of the seven variants.
The activity data guides the rational combination of beneficial mutations.
Combined variants are tested for further activity improvements.
Thermostability of combined variants is evaluated to check trade-offs.
Structural modeling is performed to interpret the experimental results.
Detailed structural analysis explains the effects of specific substitutions.
The combined variants' performance supports the conclusion that cold activity and thermostability can be integrated.
Thermostability data for combined variants supports the conclusion.

must_cover_points: Hypothesis: directed evolution can improve cold activity
Library construction: error-prone PCR + DNA shuffling, >100,000 clones
Seven variants identified with increased activity at 15 °C
Rational combination of P9S, K27Q, T162I
Thermostability of combined variants: some improved, some reduced
Conclusion: cold activity and thermostability can be integrated
